headline: MSTR and TSLA both sit in long-gamma regimes with NVRP readings of 1.66 and 1.83 respectively, creating a rich environment for premium sellers who fade extreme call skew at GEX resistance walls over 17–31 DTE.

setup.gamma_note: Net long gamma ($15.5B) from market makers dampens intraday moves and creates mean reversion toward the 170–175 GEX call wall cluster; expect smaller-than-expected realized moves relative to front IV.

bull.path: BTC sustains above 76k and reclaims 78k; MSTR compresses call skew and breaks through the 175 GEX wall toward 180–185 where the next major GEX cluster sits.
triggers: MSTR closes above 176 on volume greater than 1.5x the 20-day average
Front P/C spread narrows to below -10 pts from -19 pts

base.path: BTC ranges 74k–77k; MSTR oscillates between the 165 put wall and 175 call wall with high front IV (102%) decaying rapidly as realized moves stay inside the GEX-anchored range.
triggers: MSTR holds 163–176 intraday for three or more consecutive sessions
NVRP stays above 1.50 confirming ongoing seller edge

bear.path: BTC breaks below 74k; MSTR cracks the 165 put GEX wall and accelerates toward the 155–150 persistent put support cluster where put OI is heaviest.
triggers: MSTR closes below 163 on above-average volume
Front ATM IV spikes above 115% signaling panic hedging

trades.rationale: Fade call skew at the 175 GEX call wall — the highest net gamma node at $1.29B — where short-dated speculative call flow (87.4% of total calls) is likely to exhaust; NVRP 1.66 confirms front IV at 102% is significantly rich versus the 30-day RV of 61.5%.
trades.exit: Buy back at 50% credit capture ($1.90); stop if MSTR closes above 178 on two consecutive sessions or front ATM IV spikes above 120%.

trades.rationale: Collect put premium anchored inside the long-gamma floor; the 145–155 zone contains three of the five largest GEX put walls (ranging from -$283M to -$344M each), providing structural support; 31 DTE positions theta decay to accelerate through May expiry without excessive early-gamma risk.
trades.exit: Buy back at 50% credit capture ($1.10); stop if MSTR spot closes below 158 on a confirmed BTC correlation break or if net GEX flips negative.

risks: BTC correlation: a gap down in BTC can pull MSTR through the 165 put wall before market makers can re-hedge, triggering a gamma cascade that amplifies the move.
IV compression risk: NVRP ranged 1.40–1.76 this week; a mean reversion toward 1.0 (buyer edge) would erode the short-vega edge and may require early exits.
Speculative squeeze: 95k+ new call contracts were added in the Apr 24 expiry this week; if BTC accelerates above 78k, a short-squeeze could push MSTR through the 175 GEX wall rapidly.
Small skew percentile sample (n=5) makes the 60th-percentile reading unreliable; treat regime signals as directional guidance, not precise probability calibration.
exits: Close all MSTR positions if net GEX (currently +$15.5B) turns negative intraday — signals a gamma regime flip and the onset of higher realized volatility.
Roll the 175/185 call spread up and out two weeks if MSTR approaches 173 with 10 or more DTE remaining.
Close the put credit spread immediately if BTC prints a daily close below 72,000.

setup.gamma_note: Exceptionally strong long-gamma positioning ($26.2B net GEX) creates a gravitational pin between the 390 put wall and 400 call wall; spot at 392.5 is essentially sandwiched with only 0.64% to the nearest put support and 1.91% to the dominant call resistance.

bull.path: TSLA holds the 390 put wall, absorbs the 400 call wall resistance, and squeezes call shorts toward the 410–420 GEX cluster; the 410 strike carries $4.81B in GEX and would be the next meaningful ceiling.
triggers: TSLA daily close above 402 with put/call ratio below 1.2
Net call OI increases at 405–415 strikes on back-to-back sessions
NVRP compresses below 1.5 as realized volatility catches up to implied

base.path: TSLA oscillates around the 395–400 GEX magnet zone; the $26.2B net GEX suppresses range expansion and keeps the stock pinned between 385 and 405 through mid-May expiry.
triggers: TSLA stays within 385–405 for three or more consecutive days
NVRP remains above 1.6 confirming sustained premium-selling edge
Front P/C spread stays negative but contained between -2 and -5 pts

bear.path: The 390 put wall fails on macro or company-specific news; TSLA accelerates through 380 (largest put GEX at -$3.5B) and tests the 360–370 support cluster where persistent put OI is concentrated.
triggers: Deep OTM puts added Apr 20 (30k contracts at 140, 20.5k at 240) convert from hedge to directional pressure on a macro selloff

trades.rationale: Fade call skew at the 405–410 GEX concentration zone ($4.48B at 405, $4.81B at 410); NVRP 1.83 is the highest reading of the 5-day observation window, confirming IV at 81.9% is significantly rich versus 30-day RV of 44.7%; 85% short-dated call flow is speculative and unlikely to sustain a push above this GEX ceiling.
trades.exit: Buy back at 50% credit capture ($1.40); stop if TSLA closes above 408 on volume greater than 1.5x the 20-day average.

trades.rationale: Sell put premium anchored by the 370–380 put wall cluster; the 380 strike carries -$3.5B GEX (largest put wall) and 370 adds -$1.97B — combined structural support creates a strong floor; persistent 370-380 put volume (168k and 289k total contracts respectively across observation window) confirms institutional anchoring at these levels.
trades.exit: Buy back at 50% credit capture ($1.25); stop if TSLA closes below 382 on closing print or if net GEX falls below $15B.

trades.rationale: Full GEX-range structure exploiting the $26.2B gravitational pin: sell the 405/415 call spread at the upper GEX resistance cluster and the 375/385 put spread at the lower GEX support cluster; NVRP 1.83 makes both call and put premium rich versus realized volatility on both wings simultaneously.
trades.exit: Buy back entire condor at 50% aggregate credit ($2.30); defend individual legs independently if spot approaches either short strike with 15 or more DTE remaining; close the full position if net GEX drops below $10B.

risks: The 390 put wall (-$1.91B GEX) is only 0.64% below current spot; a single bad tick or gap open below 389 could trigger a rapid cascading move through the wall cluster before hedging can offset it.
Unusual institutional tail hedging: 30k contracts added at the 140 strike and 20.5k at 240 on April 20 suggest large holders are positioning for a severe downside scenario.

NVRP spike from 1.08 on Apr 16 to 1.83 on Apr 20 in four sessions may be related to an upcoming catalyst (earnings, macro); if it is event-driven IV, a crush post-event would remove premium-selling edge.
2w bucket P/C spread oscillated from -0.22 to +1.05 to -0.79 within a single session on April 20, indicating unstable short-term positioning — directional bias can reverse quickly.
exits: Close all TSLA positions immediately if net GEX ($26.2B) drops below $10B — signals a rapid hedging unwind and the onset of a higher-volatility regime.
Roll the put credit spread down by one strike width if TSLA closes below 386 with more than 10 DTE remaining on the position.
Close the iron condor call side immediately if TSLA gaps open above 402; manage the put side independently using the 382 stop level.
